Overview Request flow HTTP API Storage Roadmap

crate-html v0.1.0-dev

crate-html is local HTML hosting for AI coding agents. An agent (Claude Code, Pi, anything that can shell out) takes a directory of static HTML and runs crate push ./dir name. It comes back with http://localhost:7777/name/. The human opens that URL and sees the rendered artifact — a plan, an explainer, a code review — instead of skimming raw markdown in a chat window.

That's the whole pitch. The rest of this document explains how it's built.

Two binaries, one module

crate
—HTTP→
crated
—writes→
$XDG_DATA_HOME
/crate/sites/

crate is the CLI agents invoke. crated is a small HTTP daemon that owns site storage. They live in one Go module (github.com/Twistedgrim/crate-html) and share an internal/wire package that defines the API contract once for both sides.

BinaryResponsibilityTalks to
crateCLI: push, ls, rm, open, statuscrated over HTTP
cratedHTTP server: API + static servefilesystem
Why two binaries instead of one? Agents call crate push; humans open URLs in a browser. Those are separate processes with different lifetimes. The daemon outlives any single agent invocation, so splitting them maps onto reality. The cost is one extra binary to ship and one extra concept ("is the daemon running?") to keep in your head.

What happens on crate push ./site foo

  1. CLI reads config.yaml for base URL + bearer token.
  2. CLI walks ./site and starts streaming a tar archive into an io.Pipe.
  3. The other end of the pipe is the body of a PUT /api/sites/foo request.
  4. Daemon extracts the tar into a staging directory next to the site dir.
  5. Daemon atomically renames the existing site aside, renames staging into place, deletes the old copy.
  6. Daemon responds with { site, url }. CLI prints the URL.

See request flow for the diagram.

What's in this repo

cmd/crate/                CLI (kong) — push, ls, rm, open, status
cmd/crated/               daemon
internal/wire/            HTTP request/response types — the contract
internal/config/          XDG-backed config loader (token, port)
internal/storage/         filesystem ops + tar in/out + atomic swap
internal/server/          net/http handlers
internal/cliclient/       HTTP client used by `crate`
.claude/skills/crate-push/  Claude Code skill wrapping `crate push`

What's deliberately not in v0

  • No Docker. No Caddy. No Tailscale. Those are clean layers to add later — see roadmap.
  • No database. Sites are plain directories on disk.
  • No vhost routing. Path-based (/<site>/) only — it's all localhost in v0.
  • No TLS. crated binds 127.0.0.1:7777 and speaks HTTP.
crate-html · local HTML hosting for AI coding agents · what's next
